entries.definition:
m–n–w/y fate, to fate, death, to afflict, to test; to
approximate; to hope for, to imagine, to implore; to fabricate, to
lie; to shed, to flow, semen, sperm; to recite; to reward; to await;
a dry measure. Of this root, eight forms occur 22 times in the
Qur’an, مَنَّى mannā twice; أَمْنَى ʾamnā once; تُمْنَى tumnā twice; تَمَنَّى
tamannā nine times; أُمْنِيَّة ʾumniyyatun once; أَمَانِيّ ʾamāniyy five
times; مَنِيّ maniyy once and مَنَاة manātun once.

مَنَّى mannā i [v. II, trans] to create desires in, to plant
expectations in (4:120) يَعِدُهُمْ وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا
he makes them promises and raises hopes in them, but Satan’s
promises are nothing but delusion.

أَمْنَى ʾamnā [v. IV, trans.] to pour forth sperm (56:58) أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ مَا
تُمْنُونَ أَأَنْتُمْ تَخْلُقُونَهُ أَمْ نَحْنُ الْخَالِقُونَ consider the semen you discharge–
did you create it yourselves or are We the Creators?

901

901
